FAIR OAKS RECREATION AND PARK DISTRICT BOARD OF
                                    DIRECTORS
  ONE THOUSAND FOUR HUNDRED FORTIETH BOARD OF DIRECTORS’
                     REGULAR MEETING
                Agenda for January 15, 2025
The regular meeting of the Board of Directors on January 15, 2025 will be conducted in person
                                        and via Zoom:


                        https://us02web.zoom.us/j/87345380207
                      US: +1 669 900 9128 Meeting ID: 873 453 80207


                           FAIR OAKS WATER DISTRICT
                              10326 FAIR OAKS BLVD.
                                FAIR OAKS, CA 95628
                                  January 15, 2025
                                     6:00 P.M.
1. CALL TO ORDER

2. PLEDGE OF ALLEGIANCE

3. ROLL CALL

4. PRESENTATIONS/PROCLAMATIONS/RECOGNITION

   4.I. Year In Review – Highlights Of 2024

5. AUDIENCE/PUBLIC COMMENT (NON-AGENDA ITEMS)
    (Any person may address the Board upon any subject within the jurisdiction
    of the Fair Oaks Recreation and Park Distict. Each speaker is limited to a
    maximum of THREE (3) minutes. Any matter requiring Board action will be
    referred to staff or committee for a report and action at a subsequent
    meeting).

6. CONSENT CALENDAR
    (All matters listed under Consent Calendar are considered by the Board of Directors to be
    routine and will be acted upon in a single motion. There will not be separate discussions
    of these items unless a request is made prior to the time the Board considers a motion to
    approve).

8. PUBLIC HEARINGS

          (Persons who wish to speak on Public Hearing items listed on the agenda will be
          heard when the Public Hearing is opened, except for public hearing items
          previously heard and closed to public comment. After the public has commented,
          the item is closed to further public comment and brought to the Board level for
          discussion and action. Further comment from the audience will not be received
          unless requested by the Board. No public hearing will commence after 9:00 P.M.
          unless approved by the majority of the Board of Directors.)


  8.I. Discussion And Possible Action On Matters Related To FY 24-25 Fee Schedule
       Proposal.


                            l   Adopt Resolution No. 011525-01 revising the fee schedule,
                                approving new fees and giving the District Administrator
                                authority to waive or reduce program and facility rental fees.

I, Michael J. Aho, District Administrator of the Fair Oaks Recreation and Park District, do
hereby certify that this agenda has been posted at 10326 Fair Oaks Blvd, Fair Oaks, California
and 4150 Temescal Street, Fair Oaks, California at least 72 hours prior to the meeting of the
Board of Directors in accordance with Government Code Section 54950.5, the Ralph M. Brown
Act.


If you need a disability related accommodation to participate in these meetings, please
contact the Park District Office at (916) 966-1036 (voice) or (916) 966-9863 (fax).


To watch the meeting live on Facebook, please
visit: https://www.facebook.com/FORPD/live_videos

   320 hours (as identified within the table above) which will be compensated at 50% of the employees current hourly rate of pay.
However, upon retirement from the District, a Regular Full-Time employee December choose to utilize their accrued Sick Leave toward
CalPERS service credit with no maximum limit. Employees whose Sick Leave hours exceed 320 hours are noted but the hours in excess
of 320 not calculated as a liability in the table above. A Regular Part-Time employee will be compensated for a maximum of 160 hours
                                    of sick leave at 50% of the employees rate of pay upon termination.

this revision is to allow the installing contractor to properly price PCO 075, clarifying that the scope of work should not include re-work of installed pathway
in the Clubhouse Main Level.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        GENERAL NOTES:
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     1.   REFER TO ARCHITECTURAL DRAWINGS FOR ALL BUILDING PLANS
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          AND DIMENSIONS.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     2.   ALL EQUIPMENT SHOWN ON CONSTRUCTION DOCUMENTS IS TO
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          BE NEW, CONTRACTOR FURNISHED/CONTRACTOR INSTALLED
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          UNLESS SPECIFIED ON DRAWINGS AND/OR SCHEDULES.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     3.   ALL ELECTRIFIED ACCESS AND INTRUSION SYSTEMS MUD RINGS
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          SHALL BE PAINTED RED FOR A PRE-SHEETROCK INSPECTION OF
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          IN-WALL INFRASTRUCTURE.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     4.   ALL EXPOSED CONDUITS AND SHROUDS SHALL BE PRIMED
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          WHITE AND PAINTED TO MATCH SURROUNDING AREA.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           QUANTITIES.

ASI V3-2.1 revises pathway that was updated in ASI V3-2 to match the as-built drawings provided by the installing contractor on 5/9/2024. The intention of
this revision is to allow the installing contractor to properly price PCO 075, clarifying that the scope of work should not include re-work of installed pathway
in the Clubhouse Main Level.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           SHEET NOTES:                                                                                  GENERAL NOTES:

                                                                                                                                                                                                                    1
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   AND DIMENSIONS.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                    2   (N) J-HOOKING IN ACCESSIBLE ATTIC SPACE.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             2.    ALL EQUIPMENT SHOWN ON CONSTRUCTION DOCUMENTS IS TO
                                                                                                                                                                                                                    3   (N) 1 EA. 1" CONDUIT, STUB INTO ACCESSIBLE CEILING AREA.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   BE NEW, CONTRACTOR FURNISHED/CONTRACTOR INSTALLED
                                                                                                                                                                                                                    4   (N) 1 EA. 1" CONDUIT.                                                                      UNLESS SPECIFIED ON DRAWINGS AND/OR SCHEDULES.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                    5   (N) 1 EA. 1" CONDUIT SLEEVE THROUGH WALL. CAULK AND SEAL                             3.    ALL ELECTRIFIED ACCESS AND INTRUSION SYSTEMS MUD RINGS
                                                                                                                                                                                                                        AS REQUIRED BY WALL RATING. SEE ARCHITECTURAL DRAWINGS                                     SHALL BE PAINTED RED FOR INSPECTION PRIOR TO CABLING
                                                                                                                                                                                                                        FOR WALL RATINGS.                                                                          INSTALLATION..
                                                                                                                                                                                                                    6   (N) SURFACE MOUNT WIREMOLD WM2300 SPLIT CHANNEL

9722 Kent St

Re: Change Proposal 1720002-54 for Request for Quote #017: CE #081 - V3 ASI 002 Low Voltage
Revisions

Project: Village Park Renovation (T)

Village Park
Village Park, 7991 California Ave, Fair Oaks, CA 95628


This Proposal is for the Change referenced above and more particularly defined by the Scope of Work
comprised of this Proposal, its Attachments, and other Contract Documents incorporated by reference.
Therefore, we propose to change the following:


Changes per RFQ #17 CE #081V3 ASI 002 Low Voltage Revisions

- Production of as built to assist KMM in Rev 1 versus Rev 2 updates to ensure code compliance (J-
hook) and conformity to Rev 0.
- Investigation of above ceiling lower level to advise on location of access points and feasibility of ENT
run.
- Labor and Material ADD/ Deduct per takeoff of drawings.
-Add and deduct for T2.00, T2.11, TEA2.00, TEA2.11 less work completed against deduct.
EXCLUSIONS- all work outside of DIV 26, 27 and 28 including all painting, patching, grading, finish work,
concrete work, etc.



Price:              $ 32,335.90 Thirty Two Thousand Three Hundred Thirty Five Dollars and Ninety Cents
Time:               The duration of the Work to achieve Substantial Completion will be INCREASED by
                    65 days.

Clarification(s):   - None.

Expiration:         This Proposal shall remain open for 30 calendar day(s).
Attachment(s):      Additional Attachments



Please contact me at 916-550-0075 or via e-mail nickvida@rdelectric.net if you have any questions or
require additional information.
Regards,


R & D Electric, Inc. dba R&D Electric CSLB#1044712




Nick Vida

                 FAIR OAKS RECREATION AND PARK DISTRICT
      ONE THOUSAND FOUR HUNDRED AND THIRTY-NINTH BOARD OF DIRECTORS’
                            REGULAR MEETING

                              Minutes for December 18, 2024

The one thousand four hundred and thirty-ninth meeting of the Fair Oaks Recreation and Park District
Board of Directors was held on Wednesday, December 18, 2024, at the Fair Oaks Water District,
10326 Fair Oaks Blvd., Fair Oaks, CA.

For the Record:                      Chair Carhart called the regular meeting to order at 6:00 PM.

Board Members Present:               Chair Carhart, Vice-Chair Tamagni, Director Irwin, Director
                                     Mounts, Director O’Farrell

Board Members Absent:                None

Staff Present:                       District Administrator Mike Aho, Administrative Assistant II
                                     Ian Roberts, Administrative Services Manager Jennifer
                                     Larkin, Arts and Entertainment Manager Jennifer Schuler,
                                     Parks and Facilities Manager Sean Ventura, Recreation
                                     Manager Nick Davison

Members of the Public:               9

SWEARING IN OF NEWLY ELECTED BOARD DIRECTORS
Chair Carhart swore in the re-elected Board Directors Mounts, O’Farrell and Tamagni.

PRESENTATIONS:
Parks and Facilities Manager Sean Ventura gave an informational presentation about different
options for moving the disc golf course to another District park.

PUBLIC COMMENT:
Clara Kopperund of Fair Oaks spoke to the Board regarding not being able to play in the
treehouse in Miller Park.

Lindsey Kopperund of Fair Oaks spoke to the Board with some suggestions on how to make
Miller Park safer with the disc golf course in place by removing baskets in certain places.

Steven Pugliese of Fair Oaks spoke to the Board in favor of removing some of the baskets.

Marissa Pickard of Fair Oaks spoke to the Board in favor of moving the disc golf course to
another park.

Online Comments
Rebecca spoke to the Board appreciating the presentation on safety hazards.

Paloma wrote a comment to the Board that she likes the idea of moving the course to Bannister
Park.

           STAFF REPORT

Meeting Date:       January 15, 2025

To:                 Board of Directors

From:               Michael J. Aho – District Administrator

Subject:            Nominations for the Sacramento LAFCo Special
                    District Advisory Committee

Prepared By:        Jennifer Larkin, Administrative Services Manager

I.    Recommendation
      Nominate a member of the FORPD Board of Directors to join the Special
      District Advisory Committee (SDAC) of the Sacramento Local Agency
      Formation Commission.

II.   Background
      The Sacramento Local Agency Formation Commission (LAFCo) is a
      countywide commission with the goals of ensuring the orderly formation of
      local governmental agencies, preserving agricultural and open space lands,
      and discouraging sprawl.

      LAFCo governs:
         • Boundary changes (annexations) of cities and special districts
         • Formation of new agencies
         • Incorporation of new cities and districts
         • Consolidation or reorganization of special districts and or cities
         • Municipal Service Reviews
         • Spheres of Influence Updates

      The purpose of the SDAC is to provide LAFCo with input on issues related
      to special districts, as well as to receive information on issues before the
      Commission.

      The SDAC membership of seventeen (17) is composed of the two LAFCo
      Special District Commissioners, the Alternate Special District Commissioner,
       and representatives from recreation and park, fire, water, flood control,
       cemetery, and other types of special districts.

III.   Problem /Situation/ Request
       LAFCo invites the District to submit the name of an FORPD Board Member
       for membership on the SDAC.

       The SDAC membership of seventeen is composed of the two LAFCo Special
       District Commissioners, and the Alternate Special District Commissioner,
       and representatives from recreation and park, fire, water, flood control,
       cemetery and other types of special districts. SDAC members serve 2 year
       terms without compensation. There will be 14 vacant seats and an option
       to serve two different term types:

          •   Office “A” a full two-year term (JAN. 2025 – DEC. 2026)
          •   Office “B” one year term (JAN. 2025 – DEC. 2025)

       New members will be selected by the SDAC Sub-committee on Membership
       from the pool of nominees provided by the Special Districts, subject to
       confirmation by the Commission.

       SDAC meetings are held quarterly on the fifth Tuesday, or as needed.

       If FORPD desires to nominate a member of its Board, the nomination must
       be received no later than January 31, 2025.

IV.    Financial Analysis
       There is no financial impact associated with the staff recommendation.



Respectfully Submitted,



Michael J. Aho
District Administrator

DATE: November 13, 2024

TO: Independent Special Districts

SUBJECT: Nominations for Membership on SDAC

You are cordially invited to nominate a Member of your Board to join the Special District Advisory
Committee (SDAC). The purpose of the Committee is to provide Sacramento LAFCo with input
on issues related to Special Districts, as well as to receive information on issues before the
Commission.
The SDAC membership is composed of the two LAFCo Special District Commissioners, and the
Alternate Special District Commissioner, and representatives from recreation and park, fire,
water, flood control, cemetery and other types of special districts. SDAC meetings are held
quarterly on the fifth Tuesday, or as needed at the SMUD Administration Building.
SDAC members serve 2-year terms without compensation. There will be 14 vacant seats and
an option to serve two different term types:
      Office “A” a full two-year term (JAN. 2025 – DEC. 2026)
      Office “B” one year term (JAN. 2025 – DEC. 2025)
New members will be selected by the SDAC Sub-committee on Membership from the pool of
nominees provided by the Special Districts, subject to confirmation by the Commission.
A nomination form is attached. If you wish to nominate a member of your Board, please complete
the form and return it to me no later than Friday, January 31, 2025.
Please feel free to contact me by email or phone if you have questions about this process.
Sincerely,



José C. Henríquez,
Executive Officer

           STAFF REPORT


Meeting Date:        January 15, 2025

To:                  Board of Directors

From:                Michael J. Aho – District Administrator

Subject:             Approval of a Change Order of $77,454.71 for
                     Veteran Memorial Amphitheatre Hanger Doors

I.     Recommendation
       Authorize the District Administrator to sign the Change Order (Attachment
       A) for the Amphitheater Hangar Door in the amount of $77,454.17.

II.    Background
       The Veterans Memorial Amphitheater, part of our Measure J project, is
       equipped with a substantial hangar door that fronts the stage. This door is
       integral to the facility's security, allowing it to be secured when not in use.
       Additionally, its design supports the dual use of the space, including the
       Black Box Theater, making it a versatile, year-round performance facility.
       The door plays a critical role in temperature control within the venue, aiding
       in both heating and cooling, thus ensuring a comfortable environment for
       performances and events throughout the year.

III.   Problem /Situation/ Request
       During the installation process, unforeseen structural challenges and
       changes in material availability necessitated modifications to the original
       door design. Specifically, the original 2-inch-thick panels intended for the
       door have been discontinued, requiring a shift to 1.5-inch-thick panels. This
       change means that the eight panels needed for the two doors now require
       new framing, flashing, support brackets, and weather stripping to
       accommodate the different panel thickness. These modifications have led
       to an increase in costs. The attached detailed Change Order accounts for
       these necessary adjustments and associated costs, which exceed the
       $70,000 threshold and therefore require Board approval.
IV.    Financial Analysis
       The total additional cost for this Change Order is $77,454.17. This expense,
       along with two other Change Orders related to the seating replacement —
       one for $102,200 for seating demolition and material removal, and another
       for $121,996 for electrical work and trenching — will be covered by the
       existing contract allowance with Bobo Construction. These expenses will
       deplete the existing $1,800,000 contingency allowance included in our
       construction contract with Bobo Construction. However, the contract with
       Bobo will be increased by $667,335 to fund the seating renovation, as
       approved by the Board. Once this increase is finalized, we will be able to
       credit the allowance of $224,196 for the seating costs.
        Furthermore, the staff anticipates additional changes to the Bobo
       Construction Contract and Allowance due to upcoming road work around
       the park. These upcoming modifications will be funded by the $1,400,000
       Senate Appropriation funds awarded through Senator Padilla's office. More
       details on these changes will be presented to the Board as they become
       available.



Respectfully Submitted,



Michael J. Aho
District Administrator

Attachment A: Change Order
Attachment A
           STAFF REPORT

Meeting Date:        January 15, 2025

To:                  Board of Directors

From:                Michael J. Aho – District Administrator

Subject:             Approval of an Increase in the Amount of $667,335
                     to Bobo’s Contract Allowance for the Amphitheatre
                     Seating


I.     Recommendation
       Approve an increase to the construction contract for the Village Park
       renovation project with Bobo Construction by $667,335 bringing the new
       contracted amount to $22,817,112.

II.    Background
       Bobo Construction has been contracted for the Measure J funded renovation
       of Village Park. It is important to note that the original scope of work did
       not include the replacement of the seating area in the Amphitheatre. It was
       determined later that the seating should be replaced since the cost to do
       so later would be much higher, the work more difficult, and that the quality
       and condition of the existing seating did not meet the level of the renovation
       of the facility.

III.   Problem /Situation/ Request
       At the June 26, 2024, meeting, the Board approved the replacement of the
       Amphitheater Seating for $904,935. The design cost and permitting for the
       work, estimated at $60,295, along with the seat purchase cost of
       $146,314.25, will not be included in the Bobo contract revision. These
       services will be handled by WMB for design and by FORPD for the permitting
       fees and seat purchasing. This allocation leaves a balance of $667,335 for
       demolition and construction, which needs to be added to Bobo's contract to
       cover these additional essential activities.
IV.    Financial Analysis
       The additional work requires an increase in the allowance of $667,335. This
       increase will cover the cost of the additional necessary work and ensure the
       project continues without delay. The original construction contract with
       Bobo was $21,750,000. An increase of $399,777 for time extension was
       granted by the Board of Directors at their August 21, 2024, meeting,
       bringing the total contract amount to $22,149,777. This requested increase
       of $667,335 will further adjust the total contract amount to $22,817,112.
       Additionally, funds for the seating renovation will come from a portion of
       the proceeds of the Miller Park Cell Tower lease sale, also approved by the
       Board of Directors at their August 21, 2024, meeting. The financial
       implications of not approving this increase could result in project delays and
       potential cost escalations in the future.

Respectfully Submitted,



Michael J. Aho
District Administrator
              STAFF REPORT

      Meeting Date:      January 15th, 2025

      To:                Board of Directors

      From:              Mike J. Aho, District Administrator

      Subject:           Discussion and Possible Action Regarding the New
                         Fee Schedule Proposal for the Fair Oaks Performing
                         Arts Center

      Prepared By:       Jen Schuler - Arts & Entertainment Manager

I.      Recommendation
        The Board of Directors:
         1)   Open the public hearing;
         2)   Receive testimony regarding the new Rental Rate Fee Schedule for the Fair
              Oaks Performing Arts Center (Attachment A);
         3)   Close the public hearing; and
         4)   Approve Resolution No. (Attachment B) the new fee schedule for the Fair
              Oaks Performing Arts Center, approving new fees and giving the District
              Administrator & the Arts & Entertainment Manager authority to waive or
              reduce program and facility rental fees.

II.     Background
        In 2010 voters approved Proposition 26, the Supermajority Vote to Pass New Taxes
        and Fees Act, as part of the November 2, 2010 ballot. Proposition 26 amends
        Section 3 of Article XIII A and Section 1 of Article XIII C of the California
        Constitution to provide that a new levy, charge, or exaction of any kind imposed,
        increased, or extended by a local government is a tax unless an exception applies.
        Exceptions to Proposition 26 include user fees; government service or product
        fees; regulatory fees; government property fees; government property entrance
        fees; fines and penalties imposed by a court or local government; property
        development impact fees; and assessments and property-related fees governed
        by Proposition 218.

        According to the requirements outlined in Proposition 26, the District must
        establish user fees and fees for service based on the cost of providing the service,
        which will include direct, indirect, and capital costs. No fee shall exceed the costs
        reasonably borne by the District in providing the service (Gov. Code 61123). In
       order to levy a new fee or service charge, staff must follow these requirements
       (Gov. Code 66018):

             •      Must hold at least one open and public hearing;
             •      Any action taken to levy a new fee shall be taken only by ordinance
                    or resolution; and
             •      A general explanation of the matter to be considered shall be
                    published in accordance with section 6062a of the Government Code.

III.   Problem /Situation/ Request
       The District is introducing a new fee schedule for the Fair Oaks Performing Arts
       Center, designed to comply with Proposition 26, streamline fee structures, update
       terms, and establish fees reflective of current service costs. This new schedule is
       not an adjustment of existing fees but an implementation based on market
       research and comparison with similar fees in the area. This approach ensures our
       fees are competitive and appropriate for the services provided.

       Staff initially met with Community Partners to gather feedback on the proposed
       fee schedule for the Fair Oaks Performing Arts Center. Following this, they
       presented the feedback and the revised fees to the Board's Community Resource
       Committee. After reviewing the proposed fees and considering the community's
       input, the committee is now recommending the fee schedule for full board
       approval.

   1. Board Approval Required:
         o Complete Board Oversight: Since all fees are newly introduced, each fee
            requires approval from the Board. This process ensures a high level of
            transparency and community involvement, which is critical for establishing
            trust and acceptance among facility users.
         o Fee Categories:
                 New fees – All fees listed are new and are being implemented for
                   the first time.

       The Board’s adoption of Resolution 011525-01 will officially establish these new
       fees. The resolution enables the Board to periodically review and adjust the fee
       schedule as necessary to respond to changes in economic conditions or costs
       associated with providing services. This approach ensures that the fee schedule
       remains relevant and effective, supporting the financial sustainability of the Parks
       and Recreation services while also meeting the expectations and needs of the
       community. This comprehensive and careful approach to introducing a new fee
       schedule underscores the District’s commitment to legal compliance, financial
       responsibility, and community engagement.
      Fee Effective Dates
      All eliminated, modified or new fees will take effect immediately following the
      January 2025 Board meeting.

      Public Meeting Notice
      The Public Meeting Notice for the review of a Park & Recreation Fee Schedule was
      published in the Carmichael Times on December 27th to ensure compliance with
      California Code Title 4 Section 6062a, which requires at least one public hearing
      notice be published ten days prior to the identified public hearing date, as required
      by law. The law allows the Board to hold a public hearing and introduce and
      approve the resolution by action of the Board. The fees can go into effect
      immediately after the adoption of the resolution.

IV.   Financial Analysis
      The introduction of new fees is projected to increase the Department's revenue,
      helping to offset rising costs for part-time wages and other operational expenses.
      These new fees are strategically introduced with varying start dates to optimize
      new revenue streams as they become effective.

      Respectfully Submitted,



      Mike J. Aho
      District Administrator

      Attachment A – Master Sheet Rental Fees
      Attachment B – Resolution 011525-01

                   Fair Oaks Performing Arts Center- Standard Ticketing and Box Office Fees
  For Events with paid admission, FORPD offers convenient and cost-effective ticketing services that allows for
             patrons to purchase tickets in person, by telephone and online via the FORPD website
Client Charge:

a. Ticket Set-Up (Non-Profit and Community Partner)
                                                 for the first performance                                                $100.00
                                                 for each additional performance-if, it
                                                 isn't duplictable                                                         $25.00

                                                             for the first performcane                                    $100.00

                                                             isn't duplictable                                             $75.00

b. Ticket Advance: service charge for the printing of tickets sold by others or distributed as complimentary. (for
each performance day)
1. Limit to 100                                     per ticket                                               $4.00

c. Facility Charge (for use of facility services and
Theatre)                                                            Per ticket sold-Paid to FORPD                        $3.00
                                                                          *This fee must be incorperated into the ticket price

                                                             3.8% gross sales from credit card
d. Credit Card Processing (Required)                         transactions (FORPD takes on cost)

e. New Seat Map for Assigned Seating                         If desired/Or needed                                         $100.00

Patron Service Fees:

a. Partron Service Fee, on all sales                         per ticket                                                     $1.50
b. Patron Mailed Ticket Fee                                  per order                                                      $4.00
(Print at home tickets and will call are free)

Box Office Services and Fees:
1.       Advance tickets must be paid for at the time of pick-up.
2.       No refunds for advance tickets
3.       Unused advance tickets may be returned up to 72 hours prior to the event to be put back into our bock office sales.
4.       There can be no separate ticket sales in the lobby.
5.       Our box office will only be open two hours prior to show times as of opening.
6.       If you need the box office open at a different time prior to you event, advance notice of one month is needed.
7.       Box office will remain open until after intermission, or 30 minutes after show starts, if no intermission.
8.    72 hours after your event, you will receive three ticketing reports:
                   a.       Gross Sales: Basic details of every transaction, in order received.
                   b.       Summary Statistics: Breaks down sales by ticket type, payment method and demographics.
                   c.        Patron Report: Gives you an excel sheet with all patron information.

9.    Tickets sales, minus invoiced costs, will be paid by check to the entity contracted within 3 weeks of the event, most times.
                                                                                                  Attachment B
                                         RESOLUTION NO. 011525-01

      BEFORE THE GOVERNING BOARD OF THE FAIR OAKS RECREATION & PARK DISTRICT

                                 County of Sacramento, State of California

    A RESOLUTION OF THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS OF THE FAIR OAKS RECREATION & PARK
  DISTRICT ESTABLISHING A FEE SCHEDULE FOR THE PERFORMANCE OF VARIOUS PARK AND
                                RECREATION SERVICES

WHEREAS the District intends to adopt Resolution No. 011525-01 which sets forth a fee schedule for various
recreation programs and fees; and

WHEREAS budgetary considerations have required this schedule of fees to be adopted; and

WHEREAS California Government Code Sections 61123 and 66018 authorize the District to adopt a fee
schedule to defray the cost of providing services to the general public; and

WHEREAS the Board of Directors desires that the District Administrator & the Arts and Entertainment
Manager have the authority to waive fees when it is deemed, in his or her sole discretion, to be in the best
interests of the residents of the District; and

WHEREAS the performance of these services hereinafter described enhances the quality of life enjoyed by
the residents of the District.

NOW, THEREFORE, THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS OF THE FAIR OAKS RECREATION & PARK
DISTRICT DOES HEREBY RESOLVE AS FOLLOWS:

Section 1. Purpose: The fees and charges imposed by this Resolution are for the purpose of defraying the
operating expenses, purchasing, and leasing supplies, equipment and materials necessary to maintain service
within the service area of the District.

Section 2. Establishment of a Fee Schedule: The Board of Directors establishes the fee schedule set forth in
Attachment “A” incorporated herein.

Section 3. Amendment of Fees: Fees may be amended by a resolution adopted by the Board of Directors at
a regularly scheduled meeting to reflect increases in costs resulting from inflation or the experience of District
administration of the fee if the increase is 20% or greater. For fee increases 19% or less, the Board of Directors
gives the District Administrator and the Arts and Entertainment Manager the authority and discretion to amend
said fees.

Section 4. Invalidity: Should a court of competent jurisdiction hold any fee or provision of this Resolution invalid
or unenforceable, that holding shall not affect the validity or enforceability of the remaining fees or provisions.

Section 5. Waiving of Fees: The District Administrator & the Arts and Entertainment Manager have the
authority to waive or reduce program and facility rental fees.

Section 6. Effective Date: This Resolution shall become effective upon its adoption.
